---
title: "Stippling and Halftone"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Stippling and Halftone}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

Stippling samples more points from dark areas. Halftone uses a regular grid and
maps darker cells to larger marks.

```{r}
library(ImageArtR)
img <- read_art_image(system.file("extdata", "example.png", package = "ImageArtR"))

stipple <- image_stipple_art(img, n = 3000, color = "original", seed = 123)
halftone <- image_halftone(img, cell_size = 10, mode = "color")

plot(stipple)
plot(halftone)
```

Recommended ranges: 2,000-10,000 stipple points for medium images, and 6-16
pixel halftone cells. Very high point counts are good candidates for future
Rcpp sampling helpers.
